title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
problem_statement: You have a graph of n nodes. You are given an integer n and an array edges where edges[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that there is an undirected edge between ai and bi in the graph. Return the number of connected components in the graph.

examples.explanation: The graph has 2 connected components: {0,1,2} and {3,4}.

intuition: Use Union-Find to merge connected nodes. The number of unique roots is the number of components.

approaches.name: Union-Find
approaches.description: Union all edges, then count nodes where parent[i] == i.
approaches.why_not: DFS/BFS count components by traversal, but Union-Find is efficient for this.

approaches.name: DFS Traversal
approaches.description: Build graph, DFS from unvisited nodes, increment count each start.
approaches.why_not: Union-Find doesn't build graph, DFS does.
interview_commentary: Increment count for each new start.

interview_qa.a: Union-Find is faster for many unions; DFS for traversal.

interview_qa.q: How to count components with BFS?
interview_qa.a: Similar to DFS, use queue instead.
